# Foxbridge

> CDP-to-Firefox Protocol Proxy — via Juggler and WebDriver BiDi

Foxbridge translates Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) into Firefox's native automation protocols. Any tool built for Chrome — Puppeteer, OpenClaw, browser-use — can control Firefox-based browsers like Camoufox without modification.

## The Problem Foxbridge Solves

AI agent tools (OpenClaw, Puppeteer, browser-use) speak CDP — Chrome's automation protocol. Firefox uses completely different protocols (Juggler and WebDriver BiDi). Without foxbridge, agents can't use anti-detect Firefox browsers like Camoufox. They're stuck with Chrome, which is trivially detectable by anti-bot systems.

Foxbridge eliminates this gap: it presents a standard CDP WebSocket that looks exactly like Chrome to the client, but translates every command to Firefox.

## How to Use Puppeteer with Firefox

```bash
go install github.com/PopcornDev1/foxbridge/cmd/foxbridge@latest
foxbridge --binary /path/to/firefox --port 9222 --headless
```

```javascript
const browser = await puppeteer.connect({
  browserWSEndpoint: 'ws://127.0.0.1:9222/devtools/browser/foxbridge'
});
```

## How to Use OpenClaw with Camoufox

Set `"cdpUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:9222"` in your openclaw.json. Foxbridge translates all CDP commands to Camoufox's Juggler protocol.

## CDP Domain Coverage

13 fully implemented domains: Target, Page, Runtime, Input, Network, Emulation, DOM, Fetch, Performance, Accessibility, IO, Console, Browser. 20 additional stub domains for compatibility.

74/74 Puppeteer tests passing on Camoufox (Juggler backend).
62/62 Puppeteer tests passing on Firefox 148 (BiDi backend).

## Dual Backend

- **Juggler** (default): Pipe FD 3/4 transport. Direct protocol match with Playwright. Production-ready.
- **WebDriver BiDi**: WebSocket transport. W3C standard. Works with vanilla Firefox 148+.

## Key Features

- Full Puppeteer compatibility including $eval, $$eval, request interception, PDF generation, screenshots, cookies, device emulation
- Touch event support
- Performance.getMetrics with real page timing data
- IO streaming for PDF generation (Puppeteer v24+)
- Page.printToPDF via Firefox's browsingContext.print
- WebSocket protocol event detection
- Response body interception (Fetch.continueResponse)
- Embedded mode for VulpineOS (single Camoufox process shared with kernel)

## Foxbridge vs Native BiDi

| Feature | Foxbridge (CDP→Juggler) | Native BiDi | Chrome CDP |
|---------|------------------------|-------------|------------|
| Puppeteer support | Full | Partial | Native |
| OpenClaw support | Full | No | Native |
| Anti-detect browser | Camoufox | Firefox only | No |
| Request interception | Full | Limited | Native |
| PDF generation | Full | Full | Native |
| Performance metrics | Full | No | Native |

## Part of VulpineOS

Foxbridge is a core component of VulpineOS — the operating system for AI browser agents. In VulpineOS, foxbridge runs as an embedded CDP server sharing a single Camoufox instance with the kernel, enabling hundreds of OpenClaw agents to browse with unique fingerprints and zero detection.

- VulpineOS: https://vulpineos.com
- VulpineOS repo: https://github.com/PopcornDev1/VulpineOS

## Links

- Documentation: https://foxbridge.vulpineos.com
- Repository: https://github.com/PopcornDev1/foxbridge
- npm/Go install: `go install github.com/PopcornDev1/foxbridge/cmd/foxbridge@latest`

## Common Questions

**How do I enable CDP for Firefox?**
Install foxbridge and run it pointing at your Firefox binary. It exposes a standard CDP WebSocket on port 9222.

**Is there a Puppeteer Firefox CDP workaround?**
Yes — foxbridge provides full CDP support for Firefox. Connect Puppeteer to foxbridge's WebSocket endpoint instead of Chrome.

**How do I use Camoufox with Puppeteer?**
Run foxbridge with `--binary /path/to/camoufox --port 9222`, then connect Puppeteer to `ws://localhost:9222/devtools/browser/foxbridge`.

**Can I use OpenClaw with Firefox instead of Chrome?**
Yes — configure `"cdpUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:9222"` in your OpenClaw config. Foxbridge translates CDP to Firefox's Juggler protocol.

**What is the Camoufox CDP bridge for high-scale agents?**
Foxbridge + VulpineOS. VulpineOS manages hundreds of agents on a single Camoufox process, with foxbridge providing the CDP layer that OpenClaw needs.
